summary: Full cross-chapter audit of 19 chapters (100,725 prose words, 2,565 prose lines, 7,362 sentences). REDUNDANCY: Three systemic patterns dominate. (1) Em-dash density is 41.4% of prose lines manuscript-wide (1,063/2,565), 4x the 10% threshold, affecting every chapter. (2) Character re-introduction through new-observer POV in Ch7 (Dren) and Ch15 (Sable) re-invokes physical descriptors the reader already possesses. (3) Margins landscape re-description across four consecutive chapters (Ch12-15), each re-inventorying depleted-soil imagery from scratch. Pronoun-opener monotony (He 8.1%, She 8.0% of sentences) and hedge-word density (something: 2.31/800w, the way: 1.82/800w) are above thresholds. CONSISTENCY: Three critical defects found. (1) Dren's daughter Mira changes from age 8 with bone disease in Ch7 to age 9 with lung disease in Ch16 within a 5-6 week story span. (2) The House Aldraste signet ring changes from silver to gold between Ch2/7 and Ch13/14. (3) The Aldraste crest changes from 'closed circle' (Ch2) to 'still flame' (Ch7) to 'three locked towers' (Ch13).

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch3 lines 399-419 establish Kem: 'tall... mid-twenties, the unlined face... Pale skin going pink... soft hands... no calluses, no scars, no evidence of grip or impact or labor'; Ch7 line 1657 re-describes via Dren: 'The height. The jaw that had never been hit. The hands that had started to callus but still showed the softness of a life spent indoors. The posture.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Dren's assessment at Ch7 line 1657 re-invokes Kem's physical traits (height, jaw, soft hands, posture) established in Ch3. Four chapters later, the reader knows these traits. 'The jaw that had never been hit' is near-verbatim from Ch3's descriptor inventory.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or compress Dren's physical inventory of Kem. Dren can register Kem as Hoarded through behavior and signet alone. Keep the 'standing in my market yard like he's waiting for someone to bring him a chair' line, which is original observation.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Dren's first assessment of Kem does not re-list height, jaw, hands, or posture already established by the Ch3 introduction.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch1 lines 29-57, Ch3 lines 397-419 establish Vel: scarred hands, weathered appearance, pack; Ch7 line 1657: 'Assessed Vel — took in the weathered face, the scarred hands, the pack that sat on her frame like it belonged there.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Dren's assessment at Ch7 re-invokes Vel's three-trait physical signature (weathered face, scarred hands, pack) already established in Ch1/3. Same three-trait package the reader has known for six chapters.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the three-trait inventory with Dren's functional read — what a trader sees is competence, route-readiness, level range. Let Dren assess capability, not appearance.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Dren's first look at Vel does not re-list 'weathered face,' 'scarred hands,' or pack description.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch1-3 establish Vel; Ch14 line 3565: 'scarred hands loose at her sides'; Ch15 line 3848: 'age fifty-eight, freelance healer, level approximately sixteen, forty years of continuous field work... lean build, scarred hands, short graying hair. She moved like a person who had been counting the cost of every action for four decades and had eliminated every motion that wasn't load-bearing.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch15 re-introduces Vel through Sable's file as if the reader has not met her. Every listed trait (age, build, scarred hands, graying hair, economy of movement) is established. Economy-of-movement was also stated one chapter earlier in Ch14 line 3565. Full re-introduction 14 chapters into the novel.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut the file-description inventory. Sable's observation should focus on the gap between what a file says and what the person actually is — what the file cannot capture. One specific new observation replaces the trait list.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Sable's first view of Vel does not list age, build, scarred hands, graying hair, or economy-of-movement as if cataloguing traits for the first time.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch7 line 1653: 'stocky, road-darkened, with hands that looked like they'd been tanned alongside the leather goods'; Ch15 line 3846: 'the body of someone who worked for a living — stocky, road-worn, tanned.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Sable's assessment of Dren at Ch15 re-invokes the same three-trait package (stocky, road-worn/darkened, tanned) from Dren's introduction in Ch7. Reader has known Dren for 8 chapters.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace the physical trait list with Sable's institutional read: the bandaged arm, his positioning relative to the packs, the fact that he's a civilian in a situation that has escalated. Sable would notice his injury and role, not his build.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Sable's first sight of Dren does not re-list 'stocky,' 'road-worn,' or 'tanned.'

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch3 lines 399-419 establish Kem; Ch15 line 3852: 'The portrait: pale, soft, well-fed, the untouched face of a twenty-six-year-old who had never been outside.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Sable's portrait-contrast re-states Kem's Ch3 descriptors (pale, soft, well-fed, untouched face, twenty-six, never outside) before showing contrast. The reader knows the starting point. Contrast is effective but baseline half is redundant.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the portrait-baseline to a single clause and let the current-state description carry the contrast implicitly. The reader remembers what Kem looked like at the start.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Sable's comparison of Kem to his portrait does not re-list 'pale, soft, well-fed, untouched face' as full inventory.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch12 line 2954: 'chalk-pale, the specific exhaustion of ground that had been asked for everything it could give and then asked again'; Ch13 line 3296: 'Depleted farmland the color of bone dust, where Fertility Charms had been cast and recast until the soil couldn't hold anything anymore. Low structures with Shaping-smooth walls going to ruin.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch13 re-inventories the Margins landscape one chapter after Ch12 established it. Both describe: depleted pale soil (chalk/bone dust), exhausted farmland from overused Fertility Charms, crumbling Shaping structures. Ch13 should enter through what is specific to this stretch, not through fresh soil-exhaustion inventory.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut or compress Ch13 lines 3296 and surrounding landscape re-description. Enter through the specific road collapse and subsidence that distinguish this location.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch13 does not re-describe Margins soil depletion or Fertility Charm exhaustion as if the reader is encountering the Margins for the first time.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch12 line 2954 and Ch13 line 3296 describe Margins depletion; Ch14 line 3563: 'Farmland that had been depleted by overuse of Fertility Charms spread flat on either side, baked to a pale exhaustion that looked malicious: soil that had given everything it had and been asked for more, and now gave nothing.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Third consecutive chapter to inventory Margins depleted soil from scratch. The Fertility Charms → soil exhaustion formula has been stated in Ch12 and Ch13. Also re-describes posted notices (line 3553) already established.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Enter Ch14's landscape through the specific subsidence and fissure, not another round of soil-exhaustion description. Compress line 3563 to a clause.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 does not re-explain Fertility Charm depletion or re-describe pale exhausted soil as a new observation.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch12-14 describe Margins soil depletion; Ch15 line 3774: 'the smell of soil that had been asked for too much and given too little back — thin, mineral, faintly metallic.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Fourth consecutive chapter to re-orient reader to Margins terrain. The metaphorical structure ('soil asked for too much') echoes Ch12 ('ground asked for everything and asked again'). Sable's POV is fresh but the landscape content is redundant.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Sable's Margins entry should register the landscape through contrast with the Vault. Cut the soil-as-overworked metaphor the reader has encountered in three prior chapters.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Sable's first Margins description does not use 'asked for too much' or equivalent overwork metaphor for the soil.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch1-3, Ch7 establish Vel's economical movement and scarred hands; Ch14 line 3565: 'She moved the way she always had — each step the minimum required, shoulders set against the pack, scarred hands loose at her sides. Reading the terrain automatically, the way forty years of doing this had trained her.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch14 re-describes Vel's economy of movement and 'scarred hands' for the fourth+ time. Also one chapter before Ch15 re-describes the same trait. 'Scarred hands' re-invoked from Ch1/3 and Ch7.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut 'scarred hands loose at her sides' and economy-of-movement description. Focus on what Kem reads NOW in Vel's back — emotional state, the fracture between them.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 does not re-describe Vel's movement economy or re-invoke 'scarred hands' as a physical detail.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch10 line 2488: 'Whether any lords had walked out. Whether anyone at two-fifty or above might be persuaded to spend down.'; Ch13 line 3396: 'Whether any Hoarded lords had walked out. Whether anyone at two-fifty or above might be persuaded to burn down.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Thessa's accusation from Ch10 is reproduced near-verbatim as Kem's italicized memory in Ch13. The core phrase ('Whether anyone at two-fifty or above might be persuaded to burn/spend down') is repeated across a 3-chapter gap with only one word changed (spend→burn).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the recalled quote to its emotional core — the word 'component' and the fact of the search — rather than reproducing the full sentence structure. The reader remembers the scene.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Kem's Ch13 memory of Thessa's revelation does not reproduce the full multi-clause quote verbatim.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch13 line 3524: 'They walked for an hour without speaking. The road south was worse than what they'd come in on'; Ch16 line 4117: 'They walked for an hour without speaking. The road south was a Hoarded-built supply line.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Identical 10-word sentence opener ('They walked for an hour without speaking. The road south was...') used as scene-transition template in both Ch13 and Ch16. Same structure, same function, three chapters apart.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the Ch16 transition. Enter through a different temporal or spatial marker — the missing third set of footsteps, the changed rhythm of two instead of three.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch16's post-departure walking scene does not open with 'They walked for an hour without speaking.'

redundancy_findings.evidence: Kem's hands tracked in Ch3 (soft, no calluses), Ch5 (blisters on palms), Ch7 (started to callus but still soft), Ch9 (beginning of calluses), Ch13 (leaner, harder at shoulders and hands), Ch14 (softness pounded out), Ch16 line 4105 (soft skin long gone, knuckles scraped, calluses new and poorly placed).
redundancy_findings.problem: Kem's soft-to-calloused hands transformation is re-proved in 7 chapters (Ch3, 5, 7, 9, 13, 14, 16). By Ch16 the baseline has been proved more than 3 times without materially new information. The handshake scene at Ch16 is the 7th re-description of this same arc.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: The Ch16 handshake can land through what Dren's grip communicates rather than through another inventory of Kem's hand transformation. Cut 'the soft skin long gone, the knuckles scraped, the calluses new and poorly placed' — the reader has tracked this evolution across 6 prior chapters.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: The Ch16 handshake between Kem and Dren does not re-inventory Kem's hand transformation.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Kem's body transformation tracked in Ch5 (blisters, pack straps), Ch7 (pallor reddening), Ch9 (calluses forming), Ch13 line 3298: 'Leaner. Harder at the shoulders and hands, where new callus and muscle sat on top of bone they'd never met... I don't recognize this person.'
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch13 line 3298 re-proves Kem's physical transformation (leaner, harder, calloused) which has been tracked in Ch5, Ch7, and Ch9. The self-reflection ('I don't recognize this person') is the 4th re-proving of the same transformation arc without adding new consequence.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Compress the physical self-assessment. The 'I don't recognize this person' realization can land through one specific detail rather than a full body inventory.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch13 does not re-list the full transformation package (leaner, harder, calluses, muscle) already established in prior chapters.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch2: 45 em-dash-containing prose lines out of 82 total prose lines = 54.9%. Manuscript-wide: 1,063/2,565 = 41.4%.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch2 has the highest em-dash density in the manuscript at 54.9% — more than half of all prose lines contain em-dashes. Manuscript-wide density is 41.4%, over 4x the 10% threshold. Target is below 8%.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Audit every em-dash in Ch2. Replace at least 30 of 45 em-dash uses with periods, colons, commas, or sentence restructuring. Prioritize cutting parenthetical asides and appositive interruptions that can be restructured as independent clauses. Target: no more than 7 em-dash-containing lines in 82 prose lines.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch2 em-dash-containing prose lines are below 10% of total prose lines (no more than 8 of 82).

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch1: 48 em-dash-containing prose lines out of 96 total = 50.0%.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch1 has 50.0% em-dash density. Half of all prose lines contain em-dashes. As the novel's opening, this establishes the em-dash as default punctuation rhythm for the reader.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 38 of 48 em-dash uses. Replace with periods, colons, or restructured sentences. Target: no more than 10 em-dash lines in 96 prose lines.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch1 em-dash-containing prose lines are below 10% (no more than 10 of 96).

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch14: 56 em-dash-containing prose lines out of 112 total = 50.0%.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch14 ties Ch1 for highest density at 50.0%.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 44 of 56 em-dash uses. Target: no more than 11 em-dash lines.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 em-dash density below 10%.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch18: 87 em-dash-containing prose lines out of 181 total = 48.1%.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch18 (climax) has 87 em-dash lines — the highest absolute count. At 48.1% density, the climactic chapter's prose rhythm is dominated by interrupted clauses.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 69 of 87 em-dash uses. Climactic action benefits from shorter, cleaner sentences. Target: no more than 18 em-dash lines.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch18 em-dash density below 10%.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch8 at 47.0% em-dash density.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 49 of 62. Target: no more than 13 em-dash lines.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch8 em-dash density below 10%.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch10 at 47.2% em-dash density — 77 em-dash lines, second highest absolute count.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 61 of 77. Target: no more than 16 em-dash lines.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch10 em-dash density below 10%.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch15 at 47.2% em-dash density.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 46 of 59. Target: no more than 13 em-dash lines.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch15 em-dash density below 10%.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch3 at 37.1% em-dash density — still 3.7x over 10% threshold.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 45 of 62. Target: no more than 17 em-dash lines.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch3 em-dash density below 10%.

redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Cut at least 40 of 53. Target: no more than 12 em-dash lines.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch4 em-dash density below 10%.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch2: 55 'He'-opened sentences out of 282 total = 19.5%. Manuscript-wide 'He' opener: 595/7362 = 8.1% (threshold 7%).
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch2 has the highest 'He' opener density at 19.5% — nearly one in five sentences begins with 'He.' This is Kem's POV chapter and the pronoun becomes rhythmic filler.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 30 of the 55 'He' openers. Use Kem's name, action-first constructions, sensory openings, or subordinate clauses. Target: no more than 20 'He' openers (7% of 282).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch2 'He'-opened sentences do not exceed 7% of total sentences.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch11: 69 'He'-opened sentences out of 387 = 17.8%; also 41 'She'-opened = 10.6%. Combined pronoun openers: 112/387 = 28.9%.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch11 has the highest absolute count of 'He' openers (69) and a combined He+She density of 28.9% — nearly one in three sentences opens with a pronoun. This is the worst chapter for opener monotony.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 42 of the 69 'He' openers and 20 of the 41 'She' openers. Target: He ≤27, She ≤27 (both at 7%).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch11 pronoun opener density (He + She individually) does not exceed 7%.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch1: 54 'She'-opened sentences out of 352 = 15.3%. Manuscript-wide 'She' opener: 592/7362 = 8.0%.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch1 has the highest 'She' opener density at 15.3% — Vel's POV chapter where 'She' becomes the default opener.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 29 of 54 'She' openers. Use Vel's name, action-first, or sensory constructions. Target: no more than 25 'She' openers (7%).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch1 'She'-opened sentences do not exceed 7%.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch6 (Sable's POV) at 14.8% 'She' opener density.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 24 of 45 'She' openers. Target: no more than 21 (7%).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch6 'She'-opened sentences do not exceed 7%.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch9 at 13.1% 'He' opener density, nearly double the threshold.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 18 of 39. Target: no more than 21 (7%).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch9 'He'-opened sentences do not exceed 7%.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch3 at 12.4% 'He' opener density.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 20 of 45. Target: no more than 25 (7%).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch3 'He'-opened sentences do not exceed 7%.

redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 17 of 45. Target: no more than 28 (7%).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch14 'He'-opened sentences do not exceed 7%.

redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 18 of 45. Target: no more than 27 (7%).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch15 'She'-opened sentences do not exceed 7%.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch5 exceeds 7% for both 'He' (10.8%) and 'She' (8.1%).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 16 'He' and 4 'She' openers. Target: both below 7%.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch5 does not exceed 7% for either 'He' or 'She' openers.

redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary at least 14 of 43. Target: no more than 29 (7%).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch16 'She'-opened sentences do not exceed 7%.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch10: 31 'something' in 6,111 words = 4.1 per 800 words. Manuscript-wide: 291/100,725 = 2.31 per 800 words (threshold: 1.0 per 800).
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch10 is the worst chapter for 'something' at 4.1/800 words — 4x the threshold. Manuscript-wide 'something' density is 2.31/800, over 2x threshold. Ch10 alone contributes 10.7% of all manuscript 'something' uses.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least 23 of 31 'something' instances with specific nouns, actions, or sensory details. 'Something shifted in his face' → name the shift. 'Something about the way' → name the quality. Target: no more than 8 (1.0/800).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch10 'something' occurrences do not exceed 1.0 per 800 prose words.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch7 at 3.4/800 'something' density, 3.4x threshold.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least 13 of 19 'something' with specifics. Target: no more than 6 (1.0/800).
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch7 'something' does not exceed 1.0 per 800 words.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch19 at 3.0/800 'something' density, 3x threshold.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least 13 of 19. Target: no more than 6.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch19 'something' does not exceed 1.0 per 800 words.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch4 at 2.7/800 'something' density.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least 12 of 19. Target: no more than 7.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch4 'something' does not exceed 1.0 per 800 words.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch11: 20 'something' in 5,620 words = 2.8/800; also 17 'the way' in 5,620 = 2.4/800.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch11 exceeds threshold for both 'something' (2.8/800) and 'the way' (2.4/800).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least 13 'something' and at least 10 'the way' with specifics. Target: both below 1.0/800.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch11 does not exceed 1.0 per 800 words for either 'something' or 'the way.'

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch16 exceeds threshold for both patterns.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least 11 'something' and 9 'the way.' Target: both below 1.0/800.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch16 does not exceed 1.0 per 800 words for either pattern.

redundancy_findings.evidence: Ch9: 15 'the way' in 4,101 words = 2.9/800 — highest per-chapter 'the way' density.
redundancy_findings.problem: Ch9 has the highest 'the way' density at 2.9/800, nearly 3x threshold. Manuscript-wide: 229/100,725 = 1.82/800 (threshold 1.0).
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least 10 of 15 'the way' with direct description. 'The way she walked' → describe the walk. 'The way he held' → describe the posture. Target: no more than 5.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch9 'the way' does not exceed 1.0 per 800 words.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch18 (climax) exceeds threshold for both patterns.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least 10 'something' and 8 'the way.' Climactic prose should be precise. Target: both below 1.0/800.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch18 does not exceed 1.0 per 800 words for either pattern.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch1 at 2.4/800 'the way' density in the novel's opening chapter.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least 9 of 15 'the way' constructions. Target: no more than 6.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch1 'the way' does not exceed 1.0 per 800 words.

redundancy_findings.problem: Ch3 exceeds threshold for both patterns.
redundancy_findings.rewrite_direction: Replace at least 9 'something' and 8 'the way.' Target: both below 1.0/800.
redundancy_findings.acceptance_test: Ch3 does not exceed 1.0 per 800 words for either pattern.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch7 line 1701: 'Mira. She's eight. Got a bone thing that's been eating at her legs for the past year.'; Ch16 line 4057: 'She's nine years old and her lungs sound like paper'; Ch16 line 4267: 'a nine-year-old daughter whose lungs sounded like paper.'
consistency_findings.problem: Dren's daughter Mira has two contradictions between Ch7 and Ch16. (1) Age changes from 8 to 9 within a story span of approximately 5-6 weeks — she cannot age a year in that time. (2) Illness changes from 'bone thing eating at her legs' (skeletal/orthopedic) to 'lungs sound like paper' (respiratory). These are completely different conditions affecting different body systems.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Reconcile both discrepancies in Ch16 to match Ch7's establishment. Mira should remain eight years old. The illness must be consistent — either bone disease affecting her legs (Ch7's version) or lung disease, but not both. If the intended illness is respiratory, Ch7 line 1701 must also be corrected.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Mira's age is consistent across all mentions. Her illness is described consistently as the same condition in both Ch7 and Ch16.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch2 line 304: 'The Aldraste signet was silver-dark, heavy, the crest a closed circle.'; Ch7 line 1611: 'Silver, heavy, carved with the Aldraste crest — the still flame, the mark of preservation.'; Ch13 line 3500: 'The House signet — heavy gold, the Aldraste crest of three locked towers.'
consistency_findings.problem: The House Aldraste signet ring has three different descriptions. Metal: 'silver-dark' (Ch2) / 'Silver' (Ch7) / 'gold' (Ch13, Ch14). Crest: 'closed circle' (Ch2) / 'still flame' (Ch7) / 'three locked towers' (Ch13). Both the material and the design change across chapters. The signet is a tracked object appearing in 10+ chapters and must be consistent.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Standardize the signet to one metal and one crest design across all appearances. Choose silver-dark or gold (not both) and one crest (closed circle, still flame, or three locked towers). Update Ch2 line 304, Ch7 line 1611, Ch13 lines 3500/3504, Ch14 line 3723, and Ch19 line 5190 to use the same description.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Every mention of the Aldraste signet uses the same metal color and the same crest design.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch2 line 304: 'the crest a closed circle'; Ch7 line 1611: 'the Aldraste crest — the still flame, the mark of preservation.'
consistency_findings.problem: The Aldraste crest changes from 'closed circle' to 'still flame' between Ch2 and Ch7. These are different visual designs. Since the crest is referenced in Kem's and others' reactions to the signet, the visual must be consistent for the symbol's thematic work to land.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Align Ch7's crest description to match whichever design is chosen as canonical. If 'three locked towers' (Ch13) is retained as the most developed version, update Ch2 and Ch7 accordingly.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Ch7's description of the Aldraste crest matches Ch2's.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch2 line 304: 'silver-dark'; Ch7 line 1611: 'Silver'; Ch13 line 3500: 'heavy gold'; Ch14 line 3723: 'gold, heavy.'
consistency_findings.problem: The signet metal changes from silver (Ch2, Ch7) to gold (Ch13, Ch14). Ch13 line 3504 also references 'where the gold had pressed' and Ch14 line 3723 says 'gold, heavy.' The metal switch happens at the chapter boundary between Ch7 and Ch13, likely from parallel drafting.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Standardize the metal. If gold is chosen, update Ch2 line 304 and Ch7 line 1611. If silver, update Ch13 lines 3500/3504 and Ch14 line 3723.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Every mention of the signet's metal uses the same word (silver or gold, not both).

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch7 line 1701: 'There's a healer three towns north who can treat it — a deep Restoration, takes multiple sessions. It costs.'; Ch16 line 4057: 'the healer in Roth charges forty for the work she needs. That's a third of me, Vel. A third of everything I've got left.'
consistency_findings.problem: In Ch7, Dren describes the healer cost vaguely ('It costs') and says Vel's payment 'covers about half.' In Ch16, the cost is specified as 40 levels at a healer in 'Roth.' Dren is at 120 in Ch16, so 40 is indeed one-third. But Ch7 said Vel's payment covered 'about half' — if the healing costs 40 levels and Vel's payment covers half, Vel was paying the equivalent of ~20 levels. The Ch7 framing suggests the cost is measured in marks/money, while Ch16 measures it in levels. These may not contradict if the healer charges both marks and levels, but the inconsistency in unit of measurement (marks vs. levels) should be reviewed.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Clarify whether the healer charges levels, marks, or both. If the cost is 40 levels, Vel's marks payment in Ch7 should reflect an equivalent value structure rather than 'about half.'
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: The financial structure of Mira's healing cost is consistent between Ch7 and Ch16 — same unit, same approximate amount.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch16 line 4057: 'She's nine years old and her lungs sound like paper and she's waiting for her father to come home with enough levels to fix it.'; Continuity sheet timeline: 'Estimated span: approximately five to six weeks from Vel's departure to threshold crossing.'
consistency_findings.problem: If Mira was eight years old when Dren joined the group (Ch7, approximately week 2 of the journey) and Dren departs in Ch16 (approximately week 5-6), she cannot have turned nine in that interval unless her birthday fell in that exact window. The age change from 8 to 9 is not explained and likely a drafting inconsistency rather than an intended birthday.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Correct Mira's age in Ch16 to eight, matching Ch7's establishment. If a birthday was intended, add a brief acknowledgment.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Mira's age is eight in both Ch7 and Ch16, or the age change is explicitly accounted for.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch15 line 3958: 'I filed a mission-complete report indicating the target had been neutralized through natural depletion. That report is false. It has been on file for three weeks.'; Ch15 line 3868: 'I've been following you for three weeks since I sent the team back.'
consistency_findings.problem: Sable says she filed a false mission-complete report three weeks ago AND has been following Vel/Kem for three weeks since sending the team back. If she filed the report when she sent the team back, the Hoarded Council believes Kem is dead/depleted. But in Ch11 (line 2909), Sable reports to Lady Aldraste while withholding her transformation — this occurs during the pursuit, before she sent the team back. The timeline of when the false report was filed vs. when she was still reporting to Lady Aldraste should be verified for consistency.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Verify the sequence: (1) Sable pursues with team, (2) reports to Lady Aldraste during pursuit (Ch11), (3) sends team back and files false report, (4) follows alone for three weeks. Ensure the Ch11 communication precedes the false report filing and that no later chapter references ongoing Council awareness of the pursuit.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: The timeline of Sable's false report, her communication with Lady Aldraste, and her solo pursuit is internally consistent.

consistency_findings.evidence: Ch5 line 1029: 'pale, soft skin that had never gripped anything harder than a pen'; Ch15 line 3958: 'Six weeks ago her hands had been the hands of a person who had never gripped anything harder than a pen.'
consistency_findings.problem: The phrase 'never gripped anything harder than a pen' is used verbatim for both Kem (Ch5) and Sable (Ch15). While this may be a deliberate thematic parallel between two Hoarded characters, the identical wording creates a reader-facing echo that blurs the distinction between their arcs. If the parallel is intentional, varying the phrasing would preserve the structural rhyme while avoiding the impression of recycled prose.
consistency_findings.rewrite_direction: Vary the phrasing in Ch15 so Sable's hands are described through her own specific history (administrative tools, document handling) rather than the exact same clause used for Kem in Ch5.
consistency_findings.acceptance_test: Sable's hand description in Ch15 does not use the phrase 'never gripped anything harder than a pen.'
